scenes.goal: Kaelith researches the Pallor in garrison archives, verifying Lirien's claims
scenes.valueShift: From 'I failed my duty' to 'my duty was to a lie'

scenes.goal: Lirien reveals the kill contract to Kaelith
scenes.valueShift: From 'she chose not to kill me' to 'she is trapped by the order to kill me'

scenes.goal: Kaelith learns she carries the Quietus location as kinetic memory
scenes.valueShift: From 'I am expendable' to 'my survival is structurally necessary to the world's coherence'

craftToolsApplied.T5: Tone maintained — morally complex, no melodrama. Kaelith's reaction to kill contract is compressed cold rather than dramatic breakdown.
craftToolsApplied.T6: Stakes escalation — personal (exile) to existential (world's coherence depends on her). Chapter raises stakes from local to apocalyptic.
craftToolsApplied.T8: Internal resistance shown through Kaelith's self-punishment mindset ('I am expendable' vs 'I am necessary'). External resistance via Oathbrand, kill contract paradox.
craftToolsApplied.T10: Magic rules reinforced — kinetic memory as embodied knowledge, Oathbrand as suppression, Pallor membrane physics established through Helric's journal.
craftToolsApplied.T12: Dual-arc interleave — fantasy beat (Quietus paradox revealed) and romance beat (Lirien admits kill contract) converge. Both arcs require same choice: leave together.
craftToolsApplied.T15: Foreshadowing planted — Ossya Thenndal referenced in kinetic memory explanation, Helric disappearance suggests Conclave silencing pattern.
craftToolsApplied.T17: Voice fingerprint maintained — Kaelith's short sentences, stone/wall metaphors, suppressed emotional register, counting as coping mechanism. Lirien's clinical precision, classification language, observable in dialogue.
craftToolsApplied.T18: Information asymmetry managed — Kaelith learns kill contract exists, learns she carries Quietus path, learns Pallor poisoning confirmed. Reader knew these facts earlier (Ch1-4), now Kaelith catches up partially.
craftToolsApplied.T20: Emotional throughline — Kaelith moves from 'forced alliance acceptance with undercurrent of self-loathing' (Ch4 end state) to 'pragmatic bitterness transitioning to grim resolve' (Ch5 end state). No repeated emotional state.
craftToolsApplied.T21: Vulnerability ladder — Lirien's admission of kill contract is surrender of informational control, highest vulnerability she's shown yet. Kaelith's acknowledgment that she's 'capable of arithmetic' is admission of ruthlessness, accepting Lirien sees her clearly.
craftToolsApplied.T23: Mystery boxes managed — MB-001 (Oathbrand maintenance) further confirmed via Helric's journal. MB-003 (how Kaelith walked Quietus path) opened this chapter. MB-005 (Seep's agenda) implicitly referenced through Lirien's paradox choice.
craftToolsApplied.T24: Subtext in dialogue — Lirien's clinical language masks terror. Kaelith's 'you're lying to yourself' cuts through operative facade. The 'I don't trust you / You should not' exchange establishes pragmatic cooperation without emotional resolution.
craftToolsApplied.T25: Thematic scaffolding — TP-1 (trust as structural failure) advanced: Kaelith accepts cooperation despite distrust because alternative is worse. TP-2 (price of right choice) embodied in Calwrath arithmetic applied to new situation.

sceneValueShifts.startValue: Kaelith believes her exile was earned punishment
sceneValueShifts.endValue: Kaelith understands her exile was tactical containment, her disgrace manufactured
sceneValueShifts.mechanism: Discovery of suppressed Pallor reports and Helric's journal

sceneValueShifts.startValue: Kaelith views Lirien as strategic threat with unknown agenda
sceneValueShifts.endValue: Kaelith understands Lirien is trapped by kill contract, not freely choosing mercy
sceneValueShifts.mechanism: Lirien's direct revelation of contract paradox

sceneValueShifts.startValue: Kaelith believes she is expendable, owes world nothing
sceneValueShifts.endValue: Kaelith accepts she is structurally necessary to world's survival, cannot refuse responsibility
sceneValueShifts.mechanism: Revelation of kinetic memory + Lirien's appeal to her capacity for terrible arithmetic

emotionalBoundaryStates.chapterStart: Forced alliance acceptance with undercurrent of self-loathing — choosing survival over isolation, hating that she still makes calculations this cold
emotionalBoundaryStates.chapterEnd: Pragmatic bitterness transitioning to grim resolve — accepts cooperation as existential necessity, applies Calwrath arithmetic to new context, commits to departure

planted.element: Commander Helric disappeared from records after reporting Pallor failures — suggests systematic silencing of witnesses
planted.ledgerRef: FS-018 (Ossya Thenndal mystery) pattern — Conclave neutralizes those who know too much

planted.element: Kaelith walked Quietus path 'during Legion training' blindfolded — by whom? when exactly? This is FS-006

characterLocations.CHAR-001: Duskhaven garrison, her quarters, preparing to depart
characterLocations.CHAR-002: Duskhaven, met with Kaelith in her quarters

continuityUpdates.timeline: Night following Chapter 4 tavern conversation. Dawn departure planned for next morning.

CHAR-001: Now knows kill contract exists and Lirien was sent to execute her (FACT-003)
Now knows she carries Quietus location as kinetic memory (FACT-005)
Now understands Pallor suppression is institutional policy, not incompetence (FACT-004 confirmed)
Discovered Helric's journal documenting twelve-year-old Pallor observations

MOT-002.appearance: Chapter 5, multiple references — Oathbrand burns during revelation scenes, described as wound maintained, referenced as tactical suppression not justice
MOT-002.function: Reinforces Oathbrand as externalized guilt weaponized by institutions

draftNotes: Chapter hits all three required scenes from outline. Word count 4187 exceeds minimum 3800. Voice fingerprinting maintained — Kaelith's short sentences, counting behavior, architectural metaphors, suppressed emotional register all present. Lirien's clinical precision and classification language audible in dialogue. Major revelation chapter — both kill contract and Quietus paradox disclosed. Emotional arc: Kaelith moves from self-loathing to grim acceptance of necessity. Ends with commitment to departure, setting up Chapter 6. Tonal balance maintained — no melodrama, reactions compressed and tactical rather than emotionally explosive.
